title: Absent, Weak, or Defective Intraverbal Repertoire
description: Intraverbal behavior is complex and prone to becoming defective. Problems include rote responding to questions, failing to maintain a conversation, or irrelevant and out-of-context verbal responses.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No barrier (implied). 1 score: Intraverbal repertoire is growing consistently and is in proportion with other skills, or some intraverbal responses occur but scores are markedly lower than other Level 2 Milestones. 2 score: Intraverbal errors occur frequently, prompt bound, scrolling, echolalia, spontaneous intraverbal never occurs, or rote intraverbal responses occur despite context. 3 score: Rote responding is frequent, responses quickly forgotten, no generalization, prompt bound, never engages spontaneously or with peers despite strong other skills. 4 score: Fails to acquire any significant or functional intraverbal behavior despite strong mand, tact, and LRFFC skills, or demonstrates scrolling exclusively with intraverbals.

title: Absent, Weak, or Defective Social Skills
description: Struggles with learning appropriate social behavior and suffers from a variety of social deficits, often due to weak motivators, aversive motivators, lack of verbal behavior, or negative behavior problems.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: No barrier (implied). 1 score: Social skills are growing consistently and are in proportion with other skills, or social behavior occurs but scores are falling behind other Milestones. 2 score: Rarely initiates a social interaction with a peer, emits socially negative behavior, or rarely spontaneously imitates, plays with, or mands to peers. 3 score: Stays by himself during play periods, does not take turns or share items, does not respond to peer's mands, or does not verbally engage with peers. 4 score: Has language skills but actively avoids other children, emits negative or inappropriate behaviors when forced into a social situation, or almost never responds to mands from peers.

title: Prompt Dependent
description: The child becomes 'prompt bound', where responses are controlled by additional hints or prompts rather than the correct antecedent variable, typically because prompts have not been properly faded.
scoring_criteria: 0 score: Consistently learning new skills and does not show any signs of prompt dependency. 1 score: Usually needs several trials of prompting and prompt fading to learn new skills, but the transfer of stimulus control process is typically successful. 2 score: Often hard to fade prompts, especially with more difficult skills (e.g., intraverbal or social interactions). 3 score: Hard to eliminate prompts, verbal skills are limited, and prompts may be subtle. 4 score: Prompts are very difficult to fade, most skills are prompt bound by echoic, imitative, or verbal prompts, and child may emit negative behaviors when prompts are not provided.

title: Scrolling Responses
description: The tendency to guess at the correct answer by emitting a series of several words, signs, or picture selections that have been reinforced in previous sessions.
scoring_criteria: 0-4 scoring criteria not available in the provided text.
